published_prior_art.cite: ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004Bounds: class-based missing ordinal outcome bounds specialize to the binary armwise bounded selection-bias odds-ratio benchmark

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest adjacent entries are pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 nonrectangular ordinal-chain LP, pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 budgeted trimming, and failed pid_mediation_proxy_v1 proxy-null geometry; none duplicates common-error collider ATE bounds

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest ScharfsteinManskiAnthony2004Bounds gives intervention-specific class-based bounds, not this latent (U,V) threshold frontier

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The common-error model as stated cannot generate the claimed strict nonrectangular cut: for any armwise completed tables in R_Gamma(P), take H with independent uniform U,V, set rho_d=a_d+x_d, kappa_{d1}=a_d/rho_d, and kappa_{d0}=b_d/(1-rho_d), which realizes both arms with the same H, so Theta_I^CE(P;Gamma)=R_Gamma(P) rather than a strict subset.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace treatment-specific selection thresholds kappa_{dy} with a genuinely shared selection mechanism, such as kappa_y common across d or a low-dimensional common selection function, then re-check whether arbitrary armwise rectangular completions are still realizable.

Add an explicit saturation/no-saturation lemma to Section 9: either prove the independent-copula construction is ruled out by the revised assumptions, or concede that the common-error interval equals the SMA rectangle.

Sharpen the SMA comparison by acknowledging that Scharfstein-Manski-Anthony Section 6.2 permits expert-imposed interactions between intervention-specific selection-bias parameters; the proposed gap must be a specific latent-threshold copula restriction, not merely any cross-arm coupling.

recommended_next_step: Pivot the kernel by strengthening the common-selection restriction enough to block the independent-H realization, then redraft Conjecture 1 around the resulting nontrivial compatibility condition.
